articles.title: Telangana Heatwave: Hyderabad Temperature Nears 45C - INDToday
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad, April 18: Telangana heatwave Hyderabad temperature nears 45C as extreme summer conditions intensify across the state. Several areas in the city have already recorded temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius.
Temperatures in Hyderabad have risen steadily over the past week. Earlier, between April 10 and 12, temperatures ranged between 36 and 38 degrees Celsius. However, by April 16 and 17, many locations crossed 40 degrees Celsius.
According to the India Meteorological Department, the average maximum temperature in the city is now करीब 41 degrees Celsius. This marks a clear rise above normal levels.
Telangana heatwave Hyderabad temperature nears 45C across districts
Telangana heatwave Hyderabad temperature nears 45C trend is also visible in several districts. In Nirmal district, Buttapur recorded 44.5 degrees Celsius, the highest in the state.
Similarly, areas in Adilabad district recorded temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius. Bela and Adilabad Urban touched 44.4 degrees Celsius, while Boath reached 44.3 degrees Celsius.
Moreover, Kumram Bheem Asifabad and Mancherial districts also reported temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius. Several mandals have experienced continuous heatwave conditions for multiple days.
Hot winds have been reported in nearly 20 mandals across districts such as Nizamabad, Rangareddy, and Siddipet.
The weather department has warned that temperatures may rise by another one to two degrees Celsius in the coming days. Therefore, residents are advised to avoid outdoor activity during peak afternoon hours.
Officials also urged people to stay hydrated and take precautions against heat related illnesses.
